title: Cleopatra dies
subtitle: Queen of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt from 51 to 30 BCE
fact: Cleopatra VII Thea Philopator was Queen of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt from 51 to 30 BC, and the last active Hellenistic pharaoh.

title: Jesus Christ dies
subtitle: Central figure of Christianity
fact: Jesus, also referred to as Jesus Christ, Jesus of Nazareth, and by various other names and titles, was a 1st-century Jewish preacher and religious leader in the Roman province of Judaea.

title: Alexander the Great dies
subtitle: King of Macedonia and conqueror of Achaemenid Persia
fact: Alexander III of Macedon, most commonly known as Alexander the Great, was king of the ancient Greek kingdom of Macedon.

title: Julius Caesar dies
subtitle: Roman general and dictator
fact: Gaius Julius Caesar was a Roman general, statesman, and author who was the dictator of the Roman Republic almost continuously from 49 BC until his assassination in 44 BC.

title: Cyrus the Great dies
subtitle: Founder of the Achaemenid Empire
fact: Cyrus II of Persia, commonly known as Cyrus the Great, was the founder of the Achaemenid Empire.

title: Marcus Aurelius dies
fact: Marcus Aurelius Antoninus was Roman emperor from 161 to 180 and a Stoic philosopher.

title: Socrates dies
subtitle: Greek philosopher
fact: Socrates was an ancient Greek philosopher from Classical Athens, perhaps the first Western moral philosopher, and a major inspiration on his student Plato, who largely founded the tradition of Western philosophy.

title: The Buddha dies
subtitle: Indian philosopher and the founder of Buddhism
fact: Siddhartha Gautama, most commonly referred to as the Buddha, was a wandering ascetic and religious teacher who lived in the eastern Indo-Gangetic Plains during the 6th or 5th century BCE and founded Buddhism.

title: Laozi dies
subtitle: Semi-legendary Chinese philosopher, founder of Taoism
fact: Laozi, formerly Latinized as Laocius, was a legendary Chinese philosopher considered to be the author of the Tao Te Ching, one of the foundational texts of Taoism.

title: Nero dies
subtitle: Fifth and last Roman Emperor of Julio-Claudian dynasty
fact: Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus was a Roman emperor and the final emperor of the Julio-Claudian dynasty, reigning from AD 54 until his suicide in AD 68.

title: Paul the Apostle dies
subtitle: Early Christian apostle and missionary
fact: Paul, commonly known as Paul the Apostle or Saint Paul, was a Christian apostle who spread the teachings of Jesus in the first-century AD.

title: Aristotle dies
subtitle: Classical Greek philosopher and polymath
fact: Aristotle was an ancient Greek philosopher and polymath.

title: Plato dies
subtitle: Greek philosopher
fact: Plato was an ancient Greek philosopher of Classical Athens who is most commonly considered the foundational thinker of the Western philosophical tradition.

title: Ramesses II dies
subtitle: Egyptian third pharaoh of the Nineteenth Dynasty
fact: Ramesses II, commonly known as Ramesses the Great, was the third pharaoh of the Nineteenth Dynasty of Egypt.

title: Augustine of Hippo dies
subtitle: Christian theologian, philosopher, and saint
fact: Augustine of Hippo was a Christian theologian and philosopher from Roman Africa.

title: Qin Shi Huang dies
subtitle: First emperor of Qin Dynasty
fact: Qin Shi Huang was the founder of the Qin dynasty and the first emperor of China.

title: Xerxes I dies
subtitle: The fifth Achaemenid emperor
fact: Xerxes I was a Persian ruler who reigned as the fourth King of Kings of the Achaemenid Empire, reigning from 486 BC until his assassination in 465 BC.

title: Nebuchadnezzar II dies
subtitle: King of Babylon
fact: Nebuchadnezzar II, more correctly spelled Nebuchadrezzar II, meaning "Nabu, watch over my heir", was the second king of the Neo-Babylonian Empire, ruling from the death of his father Nabopolassar in 605 BC to his own death in 562 BC.

title: Confucius dies
subtitle: Chinese philosopher and politician
fact: Confucius, born Kong Qiu, was a Chinese philosopher of the Spring and Autumn period who is traditionally considered the paragon of Chinese sages.

title: Cicero dies
subtitle: Roman statesman, lawyer, orator, and philosopher
fact: Marcus Tullius Cicero was a Roman statesman, lawyer, scholar, philosopher, orator, and writer who tried to uphold optimate principles during the political crises of the Roman Republic that led to the establishment of the Roman Empire.

title: Archimedes dies
subtitle: Greek mathematician and physicist
fact: Archimedes of Syracuse was an Ancient Greek mathematician, physicist, engineer, astronomer, and inventor from the city of Syracuse in Sicily.

title: Darius I dies
subtitle: The fourth and greatest Achaemenid emperor
fact: Darius I, commonly known as Darius the Great, was the third King of Kings of the Achaemenid Empire, reigning from 522 BCE until his death in 486 BCE.

title: Pythagoras dies
subtitle: Greek philosopher and mathematician
fact: Pythagoras of Samos was an ancient Ionian Greek philosopher, polymath, and the eponymous founder of Pythagoreanism.

title: Trajan dies
subtitle: 13Th Roman Emperor
fact: Trajan was a Roman emperor from AD 98 to 117, remembered as the second of the Five Good Emperors of the Nerva–Antonine dynasty.

title: Diocletian dies
subtitle: Augustus of the Eastern Roman Empire
fact: Diocletian, nicknamed Jovius, was Roman emperor from 284 until his abdication in 305.

title: Caracalla dies
fact: Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, better known by his nickname Caracalla, was Roman emperor from 198 to 217 AD, first serving as nominal co-emperor under his father and then ruling alone after 211 AD.

title: Sun Tzu dies
subtitle: 6Th century BCE Chinese general and military strategist
fact: Sun Tzu was a Chinese military general, strategist, philosopher, and writer who lived during the Eastern Zhou period.

title: Seneca dies
subtitle: Roman Stoic philosopher, statesman and dramatist
fact: Lucius Annaeus Seneca the Younger, usually known mononymously as Seneca, was a Stoic philosopher of Ancient Rome, a statesman, a dramatist, and in one work, a satirist, from the post-Augustan age of Latin literature.

title: Hatshepsut dies
subtitle: 5Th Pharaoh of the Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt
fact: Hatshepsut was the sixth pharaoh of the Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt, ruling first as regent, then as queen regnant from c.

title: Philip II of Macedon dies
fact: Philip II of Macedon was the king (basileus) of Macedon from 359 BC until his death in 336 BC.

title: Virgil dies
subtitle: Roman poet
fact: Publius Vergilius Maro, usually called Virgil or Vergil in English, was an ancient Roman poet of the Augustan period.

title: Sappho dies
subtitle: Ancient Greek poet from Lesbos
fact: Sappho was an Ancient Greek poet from Eresos or Mytilene on the island of Lesbos.

title: Wallace Fard Muhammad dies
subtitle: American Islamic minister
fact: Wallace Fard Muhammad or W.

title: Heraclitus dies
subtitle: Greek philosopher
fact: Heraclitus was an ancient Greek pre-Socratic philosopher from the city of Ephesus, which was then part of the Persian Empire.

title: Ptolemy dies
subtitle: Roman mathematician, astronomer, geographer
fact: Claudius Ptolemy, better known mononymously as Ptolemy, was a Greco-Roman mathematician, astronomer, astrologer, geographer, and music theorist who wrote about a dozen scientific treatises, three of which were important to later Byzantine, Islamic, and Western European science.

title: Plutarch dies
subtitle: Greek philosopher and historian
fact: Plutarch was a Greek and later Roman Middle Platonist philosopher, historian, biographer, essayist, and priest at the Temple of Apollo in Delphi.

title: Hippocrates dies
subtitle: Greek physician
fact: Hippocrates of Kos, also known as Hippocrates II, named after his grandfather Hippocrates I was a Greek physician and philosopher of the classical period who is considered one of the most outstanding figures in the history of medicine.

title: Euclid dies
subtitle: Ancient Greek mathematician
fact: Euclid was an ancient Greek mathematician active as a geometer and logician.

title: Luke the Evangelist dies
subtitle: One of the four evangelists
fact: Luke the Evangelist was one of the Four Evangelists—the four traditionally ascribed authors of the canonical gospels.

title: Pyrrhus dies
fact: Pyrrhus was a Greek king and statesman of the Hellenistic period.

title: Epicurus dies
subtitle: Ancient Greek philosopher
fact: Epicurus was an ancient Greek philosopher who founded Epicureanism, a highly influential school of philosophy; it asserted that philosophy's purpose is to attain as well as to help others attain tranquil lives, characterized by freedom from fear and the absence of pain.

title: Aesop dies
subtitle: Ancient Greek storyteller
fact: Aesop was a Greek fabulist and storyteller credited with a number of fables now collectively known as Aesop's Fables.

title: Darius III dies
subtitle: Last king of the Achaemenid Empire
fact: Darius III was the thirteenth and last Achaemenid King of Kings of Persia, reigning from 336 BC to his death in 330 BC.

title: Chanakya dies
subtitle: Indian statesman and philosopher
fact: Chanakya, according to legendary narratives preserved in various traditions dating from the 4th to 11th century CE, was a Brahmin who assisted the first Mauryan emperor Chandragupta in his rise to power and the establishment of the Maurya Empire.

title: Thutmose III dies
subtitle: Sixth Egyptian Pharaoh of the Eighteenth Dynasty
fact: Thutmose III, sometimes called Thutmose the Great, was the fifth pharaoh of the 18th Dynasty of Egypt.

title: Pericles dies
subtitle: Athenian statesman, orator and general
fact: Pericles was a Greek statesman and general during the Golden Age of Athens.

title: Pytheas dies
subtitle: Ancient Greek explorer
fact: Pytheas of Massalia was a Greek geographer, explorer and astronomer from the Greek colony of Massalia.

title: Hammurabi dies
subtitle: Sixth king of Babylon
fact: Hammurabi, also spelled Hammurapi, was the sixth Amorite king of the Old Babylonian Empire, reigning from c. 1792 to c.

title: Seti I dies
subtitle: The second pharaoh of Ancient Egypt's 19th dynasty
fact: Menmaatre Seti I was the second pharaoh of the Nineteenth Dynasty of Egypt during the New Kingdom period, ruling c. 1294 or 1290 BC to 1279 BC.

title: Khufu dies
subtitle: Fourth Dynasty ancient Egyptian pharaoh
fact: Khufu or Cheops was the second pharaoh of the Fourth Dynasty of Egypt, reigning during the 26th century BC in the early Old Kingdom period.

title: Thales dies
subtitle: Ancient Greek philosopher and mathematician
fact: Thales of Miletus was an Ancient Greek pre-Socratic philosopher from Miletus in Ionia, Asia Minor.

title: Euripides dies
subtitle: Classical Athenian playwright
fact: Euripides was a Greek tragedian of classical Athens.

title: Sophocles dies
subtitle: Ancient Greek playwright
fact: Sophocles was an ancient Greek tragedian, one of three from whom at least two plays have survived in full.

title: Galen dies
subtitle: Greek physician, surgeon and philosopher
fact: Aelius Galenus or Claudius Galenus, often anglicized as Galen or Galen of Pergamon, was a Roman and Greek physician, surgeon, and philosopher.

title: Democritus dies
subtitle: Greek philosopher
fact: Democritus was an Ancient Greek pre-Socratic philosopher from Abdera, primarily remembered today for his formulation of an atomic theory of the universe.

title: Kalidasa dies
subtitle: Classical Sanskrit poet and playwright
fact: Kālidāsa was a Classical Sanskrit author who is often considered ancient India's greatest poet and playwright.

title: Croesus dies
subtitle: King of Lydia
fact: Croesus was the last king of Lydia from 561 BC to 547 BC.

title: Horace dies
subtitle: Roman lyric poet
fact: Quintus Horatius Flaccus, commonly known in the English-speaking world as Horace, was the leading Roman lyric poet during the time of Augustus.

title: Mani dies
subtitle: 3Rd century prophet and founder of Manichaeism
fact: Mani was an Iranian prophet and the founder of Manichaeism, a religion most prevalent in late antiquity.

title: Viśvāmitra dies
subtitle: Great Sage in Vedic Tradition
fact: Vishvamitra is one of the most venerated rishis or sages of ancient India.

title: Djoser dies
subtitle: Ancient Egyptian pharaoh of the 3rd dynasty
fact: Djoser was an ancient Egyptian pharaoh of the 3rd Dynasty during the Old Kingdom, and was the founder of that epoch.

title: Valens dies
fact: Valens was Roman emperor from 364 to 378.

title: Sima Qian dies
subtitle: Chinese historian and writer
fact: Sima Qian was a Chinese historian during the early Han dynasty.

title: Hero of Alexandria dies
subtitle: Ancient Greek mathematician and engineer
fact: Hero of Alexandria (; Ancient Greek: Ἥρων ὁ Ἀλεξανδρεύς, Hērōn hò Alexandreús, also known as Heron of Alexandria ;, was a mathematician and engineer who was active in his native city of Alexandria in Egypt during the Roman era.

title: Sneferu dies
subtitle: Pharaoh and founder of the 4th dynasty of Ancient Egypt
fact: Sneferu or Soris was an ancient Egyptian monarch and the first pharaoh of the Fourth Dynasty of Egypt, during the earlier half of the Old Kingdom period.

title: Gregory of Nazianzus dies
subtitle: Christian saint and theologian
fact: Gregory of Nazianzus, also known as Saint Gregory the Theologian or Gregory Nazianzen, was an early Roman Christian theologian and prelate who served as Archbishop of Constantinople from 380 to 381.

title: Gregory the Illuminator dies
subtitle: Patron saint of the Armenian Apostolic Church
fact: Gregory the Illuminator was the founder and first official head of the Armenian Apostolic Church.

title: Faxian dies
fact: Faxian, formerly romanized as Fa-hien, Fa-hsien, and Hiuen Tsang was a Chinese Buddhist monk and translator who traveled on foot from Jin China to medieval India to acquire Buddhist scriptures.

title: Epaminondas dies
subtitle: Theban general and statesman
fact: Epaminondas was a Greek general and statesman of the 4th century BC who transformed the Ancient Greek city-state of Thebes, leading it out of Spartan subjugation into a pre-eminent position in Greek politics called the Theban Hegemony.

title: Sylvester I dies
subtitle: 33Rd pope and saint
fact: Pope Sylvester I was the bishop of Rome from 31 January 314 until his death on 31 December 335.

title: Cai Lun dies
subtitle: Chinese official credited with inventing paper
fact: Cai Lun, formerly romanized as Ts'ai Lun, was a Chinese eunuch court official of the Eastern Han dynasty.

title: Menkaure dies
subtitle: Egyptian pharaoh of the 4th dynasty
fact: Menkaure or Menkaura was a king of the Fourth Dynasty of Egypt during the Old Kingdom.

title: Mark dies
subtitle: Pope
fact: Pope Mark was the bishop of Rome from 18 January to his death on 7 October 336.

title: Saint Cecilia dies
subtitle: Roman Catholic saint, martyr and patron saint of music
fact: Cecilia of Rome, also spelled Cecelia, was a Christian virgin martyr, who is venerated in Roman Catholic, Orthodox, Anglican, and some Lutheran churches, such as the Church of Sweden.
